content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. Here, you only need to find the maximum number of parries possible.**

Chef is playing a videogame, and fighting a particularly difficult boss.

After several tries, Chef has learned the attack patterns of the boss and knows exactly what to expect.

The boss will perform NNN attacks, in order. Each attack can be either dodged or parried.

The skill level required to dodge the iii-th attack is AiA_iAi​, and the skill level required to parry the iii-th attack is BiB_iBi​.

Dodging is easier than parrying, so Ai≤BiA_i \leq B_iAi​≤Bi​ holds for all iii.

Chef's initial skill level is XXX. The fight will proceed as follows:


The boss will use each of its attacks in order from 111 to NNN.
For the iii-th attack:

If X<AiX \lt A_iX<Ai​, Chef will get hit by the iii-th attack and lose the fight.
If Ai≤X<BiA_i \leq X \lt B_iAi​≤X<Bi​, Chef can only dodge the attack. XXX does not change.
If X≥BiX \geq B_iX≥Bi​, Chef can choose to either dodge or parry the attack.

If the attack is parried, XXX will decrease by 111. If the attack is dodged, XXX will not change.


Chef wins the fight if he manages to either dodge or parry every attack.

Chef finds parrying much cooler than dodging, so he wants to parry as many attacks as possible.

Let MMM denote the maximum number of attacks Chef can parry, while also ensuring that he wins the fight.

Your task is to find MMM.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of attacks and Chef's initial skill level.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ – the skill levels of dodging each attack.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ – the skill levels of parrying each attack.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum number of parries Chef can perform.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One optimal sequence is as follows:

Dodge the first attack.
Parry the second attack. Now X=10X = 10X=10.
Parry the third attack. Now X=9X = 9X=9.
Parry the last attack. Now X=8X = 8X=8.

Three attacks were parried. It's not possible to parry all four.
Test case 222: The first, second, and fourth attacks can be parried, while the third is to be dodged.
Test case 333: It's not possible to parry any attack: if the first or second attack are parried, the third cannot be even dodged; and Chef skill is too low to parry the third attack.
Test case 444: Only the last attack can be safely parried, the rest must be dodged.
